In my opinion, the previous comments are not a true informed review of this particular product. This MODEL KIT is a finely crafted and well executed product. It is intended for MODELERS aged 10 and up. It DOES require assembly (with glue) and painting. Tamiya has intentionally designed this kit in pieces, on sprues, so that the MODELER can assemble them as he/she wishes, to be placed in certain wartime situations or diorama settings. This is a top-notch kit as are most Tamiya offerings. I have MODELED countless products from this company and this KIT is no exception...HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!!!!

Title: Revell Visible V-8 Engine 1:4 Scale
Text: The model was fun to build and my son learned a lot while helping us put it together. It only lasted a week before the moving parts on the inside started catching on each other and breaking.

Title: Revell 1:48 Spirit of St. Louis
Text: One of the first planes I am building (school project for my child) and this has been a positive experience - good instructions and nice outcome.

Title: SE Triplet Jeweler's Loupe - 30X
Text: This little item is a must for all electronics technicians. If you've never seen a deteriorated solder joint up close and personal, this is the baby to have. Always finds the break in the connection no matter how microscopic.

Title: Games Workshop Dark Elf Cold One Knights Box Set
Text: If you are expecting the get the old Cold One Models as the image shows then you will be disappointed. It is actually the 5 current edition models. These models aren't bad, but I prefer the old ones, that is why I got really excited, and seriously let down when I found out they were the wrong ones.

Title: Build It Yourself
Text: This was fun to put together and the solar aspect definitely sends the right message but the front wheels could never be secured and therefore, it did not work.

Title: Badger Air-Brush Co 200-1 Siphon Feed 200NH Airbrush
Text: This is a great basic airbrush. Simple to use, easy to clean and well made. I use it mostly for modeling and run it off my shop air compressor through a water filter/regulator so I can dial down the air pressure to 30 lbs or so. Badger makes a great product and I would highly recommend them.

Title: Union Confederate Artillery Set Civil War Figures 1/72 Imex
Text: This is not good for children, but they're great for wargaming and diorama building. These are like the Warhammer and Lord of the Ring figures. They must be cut from thier sprues carefully, and primed before painting. These are bigger and easier to paint then the 15mm, more detailed and cheaper then the metal ones.I brought two other sets,the larger 205 pieces and the 99 pieces. I'm very pleased with all of them. For children I would recomend the Marx sets, I had them when I was young and They're great too.

Title: Sky Marks Current Air Force One VC25 (747-200) Snap Fit Model
Text: I got this for my son who wanted a model for Christmas but has neither the patience, know-how, or really even the want-to-know how to build one at all complicated,(Long story irrelevant to this from here on.) this item then was perfect for and thrilled him. But...... In reality this is neither a 'model' or a toy. It is a finely detailed representation of a period version of an Air Force 1 jet liner. There are only about half a dozen pieces to assemble and they go easily but snugly together. The color pattern and insignia are already applied. They appear to have been painted on. Seams, rivets, paint scheme, all are finely detailed. Not being familiar with those details, I cannot comment about accuracy. The 'model' is made of some dense and solid material and the few parts fit snugly together and it mounts on a nice display stand. It could also be suspended from a ceiling.In summary, this would make an excellent table or desk top display model for any aviation,presidential history or other related field of interest buff. But to give as a toy? Nnnn-no. It would be durable enough but the cost of the item in relation to it's entertainment potential are out of proportion. It worked just fine for my boy in our somewhat exceptional circumstance. But I wouldn't generally recommend this for a child as either a toy or a 'model' in the sense of something challenging to put together and finish. That's why only the 2 stars for 'fun'. As an educational or display item, I think it's great!

Title: Wikki Stix Big Count Box
Text: Much better price than our local store or tv and my daughter (7) loved them, but boy oh boy, we have cats and those things get pet hair stuck to them so easily! We did throw some into our road trip bag and they are great for entertaining on a long trip. My daughter was able to follow the directions to make specific animals and also had fun making her own creations and sticking them to her wall. There wasn't any residue when I finally took them off the wall, but for whatever reason, they did do a little damage to the wood finish on her door - so paint, okay, wood, no. Would recommend these and I am sure my girls are ready for more.

Title: Wikki Stix Big Count Box
Text: We got this at a restaurant with the kids meals one night when my family was eating.They have been a life saver with my two year old! I take them to every doctors apt and even took them on an over seas flight. They do get warm and melt a bit but only if they're warm for a while. I plan on buying my son more for Christmas!

Title: Fisher-Price Laugh & Learn
Text: The Easter Bunny brought this to my then 22 month old. This is a small toy, but it was a perfect size for the Easter basket. He was able to work this toy independently immediately. For this reason, I definitely think it would be good (and more of a challenge) for younger childen, but even at two he enjoys putting the coins in the slot, dumping them, and starting over again. We haven't had trouble with the door falling off, but I find my son is gentler on toys than my nephews and some of his friends. I would definitely recommend this product!

Title: Fisher-Price Laugh & Learn
Text: My 2 year old twins got this for Christmas. They love rounding up the coins from around the room and putting them in the slot, then taking them out and starting over again. Lots of fun!

Title: YOUniverse ATM Machine Bank
Text: My son Josh had gotten this toy as a gift for Christmas. He has TRIED to use it but it would never really work. You have to hold your hand in the right spot and put the card in the right spot but here is the worst thing and most troubling, YOU HAVE TO HOLD THE STUPID CARD IN WHILE YOU ARE USING IT! We tried getting different batterys. Didn't work. Pressed the reset button and again put new batteries in. ZIt still never worked. Please, do not get your child this toy. Please DON'T!!!! If you do, you are making yourself foolish.

Title: Fisher-Price Laugh & Learn
Text: I first saw this toy at my son's physical therapist. My son learned to squat and put the coins in. The physical therapist also used it to get my son on all fours (she'd lift his legs so he'd use his arms more and then put the coins in the piggy). It was a great toy to motivate him to do to stuff. I love the sounds and the way you can learn so much from it. Colors, counting, big & small shapes, matching, open & close, in & out. My son loved this toy. When he no longer needed physical therapy I couldn't find the toy in the local toy store any more. I was so disappointed. But thankfully, a friend told me Amazon had it! I quickly ordered it and now my 2nd son (1 years old) plays with it with his big brother. I highly recommend it. Oh, and thankfully, I don't have the door falling off problem like others describe but if I did, I'm sure we could simply put it back on. Buy this toy before you can't find it anymore! The Fisher-Price website no longer carries it either.

Title: Fisher-Price Laugh & Learn
Text: We've had this piggy bank for about 3 years now. Early on his door broke, and it only takes a coin or two in the slot before it pops open and the counting starts over at one, but I never relied on this toy for teaching numbers, so it's really just a minor annoyance. The kids don't care. They LOVE this toy. The youngest (1.5 years) loves sorting the coins, and carting them around the house in shopping carts and wagons. The eldest (3.5 years) will occasionally sort them, but these days she prefers to play pretend that the coins are cookies and other creative things. They both love to dance around to the music, and will play the songs until I have them stuck in my head for the rest of the evening. Usually I hate these electronic songs that accompany the new wave of "learning" toys, but these are actually catchy and kind of cute.I'd never imagined when buying this toy that it would have such open-ended functions, but it has truly had enduring entertainment value.Lately we've been weeding out a lot of our plastic China-manufactured toys, but I think the pig will have to stay. The kids love him, and they've had such fun with him, that he seems to be a permanent member of the family.

Title: Fisher-Price Laugh & Learn
Text: This is a great toy for my 12+ month old who enjoys putting items into containers. The door does come off easily but it is so that little ones can remove the large colorful coins on their own. Cute songs and teaches counting. The toy also improves the fine motor skills of little fingers manipulating objects.

Title: Fisher-Price Laugh & Learn
Text: It takes a lot of patience for baby to hold coin to put in slot...fun sounds and songs...has volume control so mommy won't get a headache when baby plays it OVER AND OVER AND OVER AGAIN!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Very Durable and can be thrown LOTS!!!!

Title: Schylling Rubber Piggy Bank
Text: My 3 year old got this for Christmas and loves it! She carries it everywhere. It's so durable that it holds up to her. It's made of really strong plastic. And it's a place for her to put her money in rather than losing it or spending it.

Title: YOUniverse ATM Machine Bank
Text: My grandkids really enjoyed this ATM bank it's away to introduce them to saving and responsibility

Title: Fisher-Price Laugh & Learn
Text: My little guy just loves to put the coins in this bank. He's not even 1 year yet, but he's a big fan. He'll become much better at it in a couple of months I'm sure. It definitely keeps his attention for a little while. The songs it sings are very cute and it seems pretty durable too.

Title: Fisher-Price Laugh & Learn
Text: This toy is very educational. In my work as a developmental therapist we use this toy to teach colors (with the coins), the concept of big and little (the coins come in two sizes ), and we use the toy to enhace eye hand cooridination (the children have to insert the coins in a narrow slot). But more importantly the children love this toy, and they are rewarded with cute songs when they succeed in putting the coins in! The only draw back is that this toy does not come with enough plastic coins and the coins get lost easily.

Title: Fisher-Price Laugh & Learn
Text: I bought this for my friends baby who was a premie and needs to concentrate on midline work with her arms and hands. She loves the pig when she talks and sings to her. She is doing well with placing the coins. Would definitely recommend this product.

Title: BbSounds Angelcare Movement Sensor w. Sound Monitor w. Two Parents' Units
Text: This is a wonderful product, peace of mind for mommy and daddy. The best product on the market to prevent SIDS. My son was a micropremie, born at 23 wks and weighed 1 lb 5 oz, he was sent home on an apnea monitor. That was a major stress and very dangerous equipment, when my son started rolling over in the crib the cord from the apnea monitor got wrapped around my sons neck. Thank god i walked in when i did, he was safe. But that was the last time i would put that on my son. We immediately went and got the Angelcare monitor and were very pleased at the ease of use, peace of mind and security. Thank God for this Angel Care Monitor!

Title: Soothing Dreams Monistor with Remote Control, Fisher-Price
Text: While the walkie-talkie feature of this baby monitor is nice, I am very disappointed in this product due to a static issue. Nearly every 15 minutes, a burst of static (loud enough to wake a sleeping baby) emits from the base monitor unit (in the nursery). I am going to attempt to return the product to Fisher Price.

Title: 900 MHz Home Connection Monitor
Text: Well after reading some of the reviews I decided to give this monitor a shot. I was needing a second monitor do to a new baby that will soon be here. I talked to a few people and they said if I have two seperate monitors theres going to be a great chance of interferance between the two. I can monitor up 3 three rooms with the scan option, its works fine. SO far I have had no weird beeping or static. But a few of other people's reviews said it would pick up phone call conversations. Well my husband and I experinced that 2 times last night, one was actually inferferance from the Sheriffs office. But it only lasted a few seconds and didn't bother us at all, we actually think that it's funny. So now I have a monitor in each babys rooms and one in the living room so when my toddler is playing while I'm getting ready I don't have to keep running out there to check on him, I can hear what hes doing. The walkie Talkie feature seems to be fine, we havent had any problems with that at all. I have a home that is over 4000sq ft and when I'm in the computer room the range is fine. Unlike my Sony Baby call that would beep almost wherever I went in the house saying I was out of range. I hope this helps. These reason why I did not give this 5 stars is because we have not had this monitor that long, and are unsure of anything at this point that might happen.

Title: Summer Infant Day and Night Handheld Color Video Monitor with 1.8" Screen - Silver
Text: We have a 3 month old right now. She was colic and had to sleep in our bed for the past 9 weeks. We now have her in her crib. I can put the handheld part next to my bed and watch her at night. What is really great is that at night when you hear noises that sound like she is waking...you can look at her on the screen and see..nope she is still asleep! We take it out to our hottub and can watch her too.The color isn't too great on it but it is great anyway.The only thing I wish is that the screen wouldn't get too hot while it stayed on and when it is running on batteries that the you could get the screen to constantly stay on.I wish I could also buy another handheld too to keep in another part of the house.

Title: Graco Angelcare Movement Sensor with Nursery Monitor
Text: This monitor really is like having an Angel on duty. My daughter (a first-time mom) was nervous about laying her baby down alone but this monitor helped her feel more secure. Would definitely recommend this product to anyone.

Title: Safety 1st Crystal Clear Baby Monitor, White
Text: This monitor is so sensitive I can hear when my baby wakes up before she cries. It actually works so well I can hear her moving around in the crib. I don't have any problems with static either, even though I have the monitor down on the floor between the bed and the wall. I recomend this monitor to anyone!By the way, I have even brought it over to the neighbors house so they could listen for her to wake while I mowed the yard, the range is great!

Title: Evenflo Whisper Connect Nursery Monitor
Text: We got headaches from trying this monitor -- the low-droning, incessant static was annoying, and it barely picked up any child-related noises. One night, my daughter just came downstairs and into my room to wake me because the monitor didn't -- with both units at full volume, I heard nothing! We bought this to replace an Evenflo from 9 years ago; we liked the feature of that one to talk to the baby (almost like a walkie-talkie). This one has that feature, too, but it's useless because the sound quality is so disappointingly poor.

Title: Graco Digital Deluxe iMonitor Baby Monitor
Text: At first this monitor seemed great. But the battery died after about a year and no one sells replacement batteries. Graco advertises the battery as being rechargable but it really isn't. I cant even use the monitor on its base. The monitor is completely useless.

Title: Bebe Sounds prenatal gift set
Text: I could clearly hear my baby's heartbeat after 5 minutes of my first try! It was a lot lower on my belly than I thought it would be, but as soon as I found it, there was no mistaking it! They give an accurate description in the manual. A babies heartbeat in utero doesn't sound like ours, it sounds like a running horse! The book gave me a better idea of what to listen for. In a lot of the negative reviews, I don't think they're using it properly, it is common sense not to use it in a room with the TV on, and sliding it all over your belly is obviously going to give you some noise, but that's not how you're supposed to use it!

Title: Evenflo Whisper Connect Nursery Monitor
Text: I use my baby monitors all over my house. I do get some static every once in awhile but not so often that I don't use them. Very clear and I can tell exactly what my baby is doing in her crib. They were very moderately priced and I would definitely recommend to someone not wanting to spend a fortune but still wanting to have a product that WORKS! :)

Title: BbSounds Angelcare Movement Sensor w. Sound Monitor w. Two Parents' Units
Text: When a co-worker heard my wife couldn't sleep for constant worrying and fussing and checking on the baby she recommended this. It was a life saver. From the bassinet, to the crib, to the toddler bed. We had very few false alarms, and they never woke the baby (but man, did WE shoot out of bed!). They were due to A)a misadjusted sensitivity setting after switching to the crib, and B) the baby moving off the sensor and scootching into the corner of the crib. Both situations were easy to adjust for with the sesnsitivity settings. Double-plus Bonus

Title: Peace of Mind Two 900 Mhz Baby Receivers, Monitor
Text: This was the worst product I have ever bought!!! The static was so severe that my wife could not even hear my voice through the syatem... two cups and a string would have worked better.

Title: Fisher-Price Private Connection Monitor
Text: We're coming up on my son's 2nd birthday and the second receiver for this thing is about to die. The first died around his first birthday. I would have liked to use this monitor for a second kid, but it was just not made to last that long. It seems that the receivers are prone to shorts - we were not that rough on them as they stay in one place most of the time. I was disappointed with this product's useful lifespan.

Title: BebeSounds Easy-To-Hold Sound Monitor With Sound Lights and 2 Receivers
Text: If you're looking to buy this baby sound monitor, I would recommend that you avoid this one at all cost. Its just not worth it. Hopefully your baby knows how to pick up the sound monitor and call you. The only time this thing worked for me was when I was at most 1 foot away from the reciever and infront of it or speaking directly into it like a drive through window. This thing wont recieve any sound if your baby is either behind the main reciever or to the sides.If I could give this product 0 stars I would've have.

Title: Fisher-Price Sounds 'N Lights Monitor With Dual Receivers
Text: Pros - 2 receivers, the lights are easy to see across the roomCons - Constant interference, usually bad enough to disturb an exhausted parent's precious few hours of sleep. Batteries never last long. After about a year, both monitors developed shorts and would randomly switch off so that exhausted parents are finally woken by a child's wails from down the hall - NOT the sounds and lights.I don't know why I endured this piece of crap for 20 months. Now that it's finally dead, I'm going to buy something better.

Title: Summer Infant Day and Night Handheld Color Video Monitor with 1.8" Screen - Silver
Text: Do not buy a Summer monitor! It WILL break in approximately 12 months and the terrible customer service team expects you to mail your monitor in before they will send you a new one, which means you are out of a monitor for two weeks! That is a ridiculous option for what was a poorly designed monitor in the first place. Ask anyone - these monitors break easily. Spend more money on a better quality product that will last for the 2+ years you'll want to use it.

Title: Summer Infant Day and Night Handheld Color Video Monitor with 1.8" Screen - Silver
Text: Its a great monitor and we have thoroughly enjoyed having it - until the video shut down and finally the whole unit stopped working. The battery pack in it went kapfloie and our daughter is only 5 weeks old! The monitor cannot be used while hooked up to AC - it works while on battery mode only. It must be charged regularly, and after a short time the batteries could no longer hold a charge. If you open the back of the monitor to check the battery pack it clearly states, "Use only original battery pack. Warranty void if other batteries are used." A replacement battery pack is $30-$35, but I don't want to have to buy a replacement every 2 months for a warranty-less monitor!?! That would be the price of a whole new monitor over the course of a year! Grumble.

Title: Summer Infant Day and Night Handheld Color Video Monitor with 1.8" Screen - Silver
Text: I have a very independant and smart almost-3 year old and another on the way in 3 weeks. We live in a detatched 2-story guest house on my parents property. They generously do a lot of babysitting for me while my husband works and I go to dr. appointments EVERY day! One day while listening to the old audio baby monitor (i was at the dr.) my mom didnt hear my son wake up and walk out of our house and head down the 1/4 mile long driveway. Luckily, before my mom even knew he was missing, our neighbors saw my son and brought him home! This traumatized everybody and so we decided a video monitor was absolutely neccessary to have now.We have had this for a week and it has been GLORIOUS! The reception is great and the picture is so clear! I love all the options for picture clearity and volume. The picture does turn off afte a few minutes and i assume that it to save the battery power. this is easily remedies by quickly turning it off and back on. even when there is no picture the sound is still on. I cannot say enough good things about this product and everybody in our household is so relieved to have it!

Title: Safety 1st 900 Mhz Sight And Sound Nursery Monitor System
Text: We purchased the baby quiet sounds monitor by summer infants and we had to return it twice. The picture was awful we could never get a steady picture I tried and tried my husband said dont waste your time it just doenst work. So when we went back to the store we decided to try this monitor and absolutely loved it. I finally slept, the picture is awesome and the sound is great. We havent had it long but I hope it works well in the future. I guess when it comes to merchandise they make so many brands because everything works differently for everyone! Its worth spending the extra $50 to have one work right out of the box

Title: 900 MHz Home Connection Monitor
Text: We monitor 2 children. Rather than scan, as some have mentioned, for a while we put both receivers by the bed at night and put one on A and one on B, so we got continuous monitoring of both children.But we do get the POPPING noise! So now I also have a cheap Safety 1st ($16) monitor in the baby's room-it works great...and for my 3 year old, we use this one but if it is popping, I turn it down pretty low because he will yell or come to my room.Love that it recharges! The cheapy I have in the baby's room does not, it has to be plugged in...so if we are watching tv in the den, I use this one to monitor her.So overall I have been satified. I tried many other monitors trying to find 2 that would work together. That was like looking for a needle in a haystack. I did have one Sony 27 channel that I liked, and it worked with our original that we used with our first child, but it often beeped in my room saying it was out of range. So I took it back and got this one.I am having a problem that has just started, the sound shorts out on one of the receivers...if I jiggle the volume dial it comes back but goes out again in the night. I am going to contact the company.

Title: Sing Along CD Player
Text: I bought this for my granddaughter who is 2. She loves to listen to her music and dance and she has the songs memorized. If she likes it, she dances, if not she will shake her head and say no meaning put another song on. We have had no problems with this item and she takes it everywhere she goes. It skips if you drop it but what CD player doesn't. I would definitely buy this again and will if anything ever happens to this one. Love it and so does Avery.

Title: Disney Cars Mix Stick
Text: LOVE IT!! I bought it for my niece for her 7th birthday and loaded all her CD's on to it before I gave it to her. I tried it out until her birthday and almost didn't give it to her, I wanted to keep it for myself! It's easy to load music on or you can buy the mix clips to play on it. My niece loves it too!

Title: Sing Along CD Player
Text: I bought the Sing Along CD Player for each of my young granddaughters, 3 and 4. They love it. It is durable, easy to carry around, easy to play alone or with others. I bought it so that they could do the singing and dancing that they enjoy on their own when they chose to do it. I think it is a great toy, more expensive than the one I bought for the older kids, but a good value.

Title: Fisher Price Star Station Entertainment System
Text: My 17 month old and four year old received this for Christmas. My 17 month old loves it. She loves to dance and always wants to play with it. It is worth having just to see her play with it. My son isn't as interested and it doesn't hold his attention for long. The picture quality is fine. I don't expect movie quality in a kids toy. There are two big negatives to this toy though:One it doesn't come with a power cord. I spent a long time looking for the cord thinking I must have misplaced it. I am surprised that Fisher-Price did not include a cord especially at this price point. I solved the problem by using rechargeable batteries.Two the cartridge that comes with it is pretty bad. They have included pop mixes of regular kids songs. The kids don't recognize them and that doesn't help keep their attention. We get around this by using another source for music.In summary I love it for my 17 month old daughter but she is way outside the age range. Video and audio quality are fine. The cartridge is disappointing and battery only operation is annoying. Someone in another review suggested using a video camera hooked to a TV to achieve the same result. I guess with all of the work a-rounds that would be just as good of an option.

Title: Disney Mix Stick Chrome MP3 Player
Text: Overall, I think it's a good idea. Nice concept for kids. My boys are 6 and 7, and they each got one for Christmas.Two annoying things about the players:1 - the detachable caps. In order to charge or download music, the cap must be removed. There's no way to keep it attached to the player. This is a fairly critical flaw for something made for *kids*.2 - so far - play time is NOT 8 hours. Maybe we have a defective "charger" (the disney speaker stand was purchased also, and it seems defective). The MP3 player only played for 3 hours total - mostly on the stand, and only about 10 minutes off the stand before the player stopped working.Invest in a USB wall charger.I'm also NOT a fan of Disney's Mix Clips. So I won't waste my money on those. The player holds enough music for kids, and I find our Veggie Tales collection enough to keep the boys happy.Think about it before you buy it. It's "Okay".

Title: Fisher Price Star Station Entertainment System
Text: Great idea needs a lot of improvement.Poor picture. Microphone is a joke. Doesn't pick up your voice unless you have your mouth right on it and how are you supposed to sing when you are eating the mic. Big dissapointment!

Title: Addition Songs-CD (Audio Memory)
Text: We like this cd, it's catchy and not boring at all considering the subject matter (how exciting are addition tables?). My kids think it's great, they make their toys dance to the songs, but then they are rather creative and silly at times. I am buying the subtraction cd, then the multiplication as well. The music is non-offensive, no hip-hop, no kiddie cartoon voices, not annoying in the least. I recommend this cd, and yes, it is great for the car.

Title: Fisher-Price Nursery Rhymes CD Player
Text: Cute, solid,takes a child's abuse and still works. 3 batteries came with the purchase so it was ready to use.

Title: Tek Nek Cool P3 Player - Blue
Text: Bought this for my 3-year-old for Christmas (His teenage brother got a video iPod) He loves it! It's easy for him to use on his own and he's dropped it a number of times with no problem. Here's the deal....Great Choice for pre-schooler who loves music. I agree with the reviewer that says it's a needless complication to have the kids choose the playlist. I'd rather have 40 songs in one list. The software was easy for me to load CDs. I love that I can put whatever I want on this so I know what he's listening to at all times.Plus I can set parental controls on how loud he can listen to it. Everything worked well. No issues. Buy it!

Title: Twinkle 'n Twirl CD Player by Playskool
Text: Our little Noah (7 months) likes to use the CD Player to pound on things, so yes it is very durable. As for educational, we have learned something and that is that we need to think before we buy another thing that you CAN"T turn off... At 2 am the thing will start playing by itself. He really does like it though...

Title: Cinderella Karaoke Player
Text: I too checked this out prior to putting this under the tree. Unlike the other reviewer I was amazed by the sound that came out of this. My husband kept telling me a premium sound system would sound great and this would sound terrible, when he heard it he was even impressed.I bought this for a 4 year old and 1 1/2 year old to share. The sound seemed good to me. Just be sure to order the 2nd microphone for $9.95 if you need it.

Title: Singing Machine 5.5 TV Monitor Karaoke System with Lyrics
Text: I bought this for my seven and eight year old daughters. Bought an additional microphone so they could use it together. However, using just the machine on its own does not produce loud enough mic output. Not sure if something is wrong with this unit, or if it's an overall design flaw. I should have sent it back, but they were excited to use it so we hooked it up to the tv instead. The microphones work much better going through the television sound system. Overall, disappointed with the purchase.

Title: Tek Nek Cool P3 Player - Blue
Text: Our 5 and 7 year old girls each received this from Santa. They are thrilled and so are we. Software loaded easily and music transfers without a hitch. We no longer have to worry about the two fighting over what music to listen to, carrying bulky CD players, or leaving the house with expensive CD's that may get lost, damaged, or stolen. We can control the maximum volume at the time of download, which is something we really needed for our 7 year old. It would be nice if the unit could run on AC power as well as batteries, and the headphones are a bit too large for our 5 year old (we bought her another pair), but for $29, we really can't complain. I'm very surprised that this item was carried by only a few online retailers this year. This item is perfect for children who are not quite ready for an expensive MP3 player or IPod.

Title: Fisher Price Kid Tough FP3 Song & Story Player - Pink
Text: my three year old loves her new fisher price "ipod". She loves the pink color and can choose her favorite songs by the icon pictures(a great feature). My only complaint is that the volume at its highest level is still very low which makes it difficult for her to hear while riding in the car on the interstate while traveling.

Title: Tek Nek Cool P3 Player - Blue
Text: Easy to use, functional, sturdy, doesn't hold very many songs but changing the playlist is very easy. All in all I recommend this product.

Title: Singing Machine STVG-999 Pedestal Karaoke System with Tower Speakers, Camera, Monitor and Radio
Text: I bought this last Christmas for my kids (ages 9 and 11) from the J.C Penny's catalog. I got the whole family to chip in on one big gift.When the kids opened it Christmas morning there were no mikes in the box (the company shipped them to me after 3 phone calls) , 4 weeks later.My kids both play musical instruments and know how to care for electronics.This machine did not work after 6 weeks of use I would never buy anything else from this company or JS Penny's.

Title: Fisher Price Tuff Stuff Tape Recorder
Text: After reading the negative reveiws that door comes off I started not to order this tape recorder for my grandchildren. We've had no problems with the door, it plays clearly. I am impressed. A great buy !

Title: Fisher Price Star Station Entertainment System
Text: I bought this for my 4 year old for her birthday and it is terrible. After listening to one song, she told me it was boring. I even bought some additional song packs, but they are not music that she relates to. She loves to sing and dance, but if she is singing, she can't hear the musical recording. The clarity of the picture on the screen is not what I would consider a high quality and the star image never appears on my screen. Instead it appears even darker then the normal image.To make matters even worse is that you must put batteries in it and the instructions specifically state that if you do not turn off the device after each use, it does not turn off automatically. Therefore, you are required to turn off and turn on with each use.Most of the songs are far over my child's head and even with buying additional song cartridges, I don't think we will ever get our money out of this toy.

Title: Sing Along CD Player
Text: I purchased this CD player and I had read some reviews that it had stopped working. However, I still ordered it thinking that was an isolated case. My son loved this CD player, especially because he could sing along with the microphone. However, just as other reviewers indicated - it stoppped working a few months after I purchased it. We still have it - my oldest son is 6, we just use it for decoration.

Title: Singing Machine 5.5 TV Monitor Karaoke System with Lyrics
Text: First purchase lasted 24 hours. Second purchase lasted about 2 days. The problem was the monitor. The first lost picture completely and the second lost half the picture. My 8 year old got this for her birthday. She really enjoys it when it works. This was very frustrating. I think I'll try the memorex next time. Unless those stink too.

Title: Sing With Me Magic Cube
Text: This was another gift for my grandson's 1st birthday - he loves music, and is fascinated with the buttons on each side of the cube and enjoys pushing them - I'm not so sure he understands that he's adding instuments when he pushes each button, but as it lights up and the cube makes music, he totally enjoys the experience...

Title: Fisher-Price Roll-A-Rounds Twirlin' Whirlin' Turtle
Text: This is a great little toy. A friend got it "by accident" after she heard about my twins obsetion with peek-a-BLOCKS, but it was one of those GOOD mistakes! My 8 month olds have loved it since they were 6 months old. Its great for being able to put them in, and take them out. Soon my 3 year olds "tought" my 8 month olds how to make it work by pushing the head. It was fun for them, their first peek-a-round toy, which eventually lead to more, (just like peek-a-blocks we now have MANY peek-a-rounds and their playsets, the gumball machine, and the jungle)SO this was the first peek-a-round toy and my kids still enjoy playing with it now and then. The few gripes I have are that there is only one song, and it doesnt spin for too long, but it is still a great toy!

Title: Munchkin Mozart Magic Cube
Text: I don't know who loves this thing more, my 5 month old, or my husband and I. It never gets boring and its fun to practice your timing, by having certain instruments come in at a different time... my baby goes nuts day after day for this toy. Buy it for every baby shower! I just wish it wasn't so darn expensive or I'd get it for everyone on my baby list.

Title: Munchkin Mozart Magic Cube
Text: I gave this toy to my daughter when she was 5 months of age and she loved it and used it all of the time. She seemed mesmerized by the different instruments as she pressed each side. Unfortunately it broke last week (she's now 7 1/2 months old). Not a very durable toy. But great while it lasted!

Title: Laugh & Learn
Text: My two year old son loves this chair and has since he was 6 months old and just crawling. He could pull himself up and stand next to the chair as well as sit in the chair. The book and songs that the chair plays are not too annoying and he sang along with it when he got older. As he got older, he used the chair as his place to sit and watch a show or set his drink. This chair was a free hand-me-down but I would have purchased it given how much joy he received from it. At two, he has finally outgrown the chair.

Title: Fisher-Price Roll-a-Rounds™ Drop & Roar™ Dinosaur
Text: We bought the roll n roar dinosaur for my daughter at Christmas. She was 7 months old at the time. She didn't do much with it then, but she loved listening to the music and watching the lights twinkle. She is now 9 months old, and plays with it ALL of the time. It's one of her favorite toys, and I would definately recommend it.

Title: Hasbro Playskool Lullaby Gloworm
Text: We got "Glowy" for our son when he was 3 months old to try and help him sleep better. It has always been a very soothing toy for him, and even now, at 12 months old, our son always smiles lovingly at it when I press its tummy to make the face light up and the lullaby music come on. Whenever he's upset, all I have to do is hold "Glowy" in front of his face, and he instantly calms down. Recently, our son had to have a Cat Scan, and even though the scan itself only takes 5 minutes to complete, they have to sedate most babies because you can't move an inch or it messes up the scan. The nurse suggested we try it first without sedation, and I was really thinking we would have to have him sedated as he is a very very active baby! Once he was laying down, and they had put the blankets over him, I could tell he was scared, so I took out Glowy and held it in front of his face and he didn't move an inch the whole time the scan was taking place!! I would highly recommend this toy for any babies that are fussy or need an early "lovey". I plan on passing this one down to our next babies.

Title: Small World Express Egg-Shape Sorter
Text: This was one of my son's favorite toys. Also, a lot of the children that would come over for play-dates loved this too! I do think the box could be made a little bit better...but it lasted a long time before one of the kids got too rough with it and the hinges broke. Either way, I think that it was worth ever penny for the amount of enjoyment the kids got out of it.

Title: Munchkin Mozart Magic Cube
Text: Great toy! All of my 4 grandchildren have enjoyed it. They loved the lights and the music. Each side is different instrument and then one is the whole orchestra playing the same melody. Mozart's music is a wonderful change from the usual annoying tunes on most children's toys.

Title: Ocean Wonders Fishbowl
Text: I got my son this toy for Christmas and he loves it, the only problem is that I have had to replace the batteries over this past weekend, but he loves it anyway!!!

Title: International Playthings Flash Beat Drum
Text: The best part is this toy is fun even when you turn off the switch. My little one smacks it, rolls and crawls with it all the things she'd normally do with it when its all lit up and playing the music. So if you want quiet time, less music, this toy work great. It is light enough so even if she lifts and somehow bummps her head to it, doesn't get much hurt. btw, the music is soft too, not very loud and noisy!

Title: Fisher-Price Step & Play Piano
Text: Before I bought this toy, I read a lot of reviews on Amazon and everyone seemed to like this toy. Apparently babies played by themselves for hours sometimes. After reading those reviews I even felt guilty that I had not purchased the step-and-play sooner. But it was a different story after I bought the toy. My son does not play for more than 15 minutes in it. I prefer keeping him in the step-and-play for a short while when I cannot watch him continously instead of leaving him on the floor. But otherwise I don't think it has been worth the money for me. I guess every kid is different so what is liked by one may not be enjoyed by others.

Title: Little Tikes Baby Tap A Tune Piano
Text: This is a good, basic toy to add to your collection of baby toys. All three of my kids have enjoyed playing with it.

Title: Neurosmith - Music Blocks with Mozart Music Cartridge
Text: My 3 1/2 year old daughter and 5 year old son can not get enough of this marvelous toy. Even dad says he can listen to it all day! I purchased the Mozart and Cinderella cartridges and have found the favorite to be Mozart for the children. They spend hours arranging and rearranging the blocks to produce different instrument sounds from Mozart's Night Music. This is a toy I keep out for the chidren to play since they are always returning to it for more music and composition. This is a must for this age group. It is exciting to find the "perfect toy" that makes memories.

Title: Fisher-Price Roll-a-Rounds™ Drop & Roar™ Dinosaur
Text: Okay, I bought this last year for my then 1-year-old. At the time he wasn't walking and he would try to use it to pull up on. It always toppled over. And since he's become mobile, he'd rather climb on it than use it the way it is meant to be used. He doesn't bother with the balls and just pushes the buttons to make the lights and songs turn on. All that said, for $[...] or so, it's not a bad buy. But now they are asking $[...] for these. That's just absolutely ridiculous. Totally a rip-off. If you go on ebay or craigslist, you can find them for $[...]. And they are easy to find.

Title: Hohner Kids / 5" Handled Cage Bell, Colors Vary
Text: My 8 month old granddaughter knew what to do with this immediately and when she started shaking the bell she broke out in a big smile. It is one of her favorite items to play with and the noise is not annoying at all.

Title: Fisher-Price Step & Play Piano
Text: I purchased the Step and Play Piano for my daughter when she was 6 months old. From the first time she used it she loved it!! She immediately began playing with the different activities and it gives her the freedom to stand by herself. She is also quickly learning to balance herself on her feet. The only drawback is the permanent assembly because of the huge size. I would recommend this toy to anyone!!

Title: Fisher-Price Roll-a-Rounds™ Drop & Roar™ Dinosaur
Text: I feel like I'm missing something - where do you keep the balls when it's not in use? They seem to disappear under couches, etc. when not actively being played with. This toy does too little to be worth the space it takes up and the money...get a shape sorter or something similar instead!

Title: Munchkin Mozart Magic Cube
Text: My daughter got this toy before she could crawl. She liked the colored lights and to watch us make it play music. Eventually she learned to turn it on by kicking her feet. Later she banged on it and was fascinated when she could make it play. Now she has mastered it and likes to push it around the floor. You are going to be disappointed if you are expecting your child to learn anything about music from this toy, but it has been great to watch how her play with this toy progressed as our baby's motor and cognitive skills develop.It's been very sturdy. At first it made some weird sounds, but that went away. The music is OK, but we didn't get the toy for our enjoyment. It's fine for baby. The only negative -we had the same trouble other reviews have mentioned with getting that crazy wire out of the toy. It took lots of tugging with pliers!

Title: Munchkin Mozart Magic Cube
Text: Instead of giving little outfits that will be outgrown in a few weeks or months, why not this music cube that will keep the little one entertained for years to come.

Title: La Cross Crystal Nail File - 1 Ea
Text: Back in my college days when I dabbled in playing the guitar, my teacher taught me finger picking. The most important thing about finger picking is to have strong, well shaped fingernails (since they serve as your 'picks'. And it was my guitar teacher who pointed me towards crystal nail files.The price put me off at first, but under his assurance that I would love them, I went ahead and took the plunge. And he was right -- all other files pale by comparison. Even though my guitar is now abandoned in my closet, I still use these files.The grain on the files goes from coarse to fine, so that you don't have to move from one file to another, and they give a wonderful, smooth finish for an instant manicure. Having such a gentle, high quality file for my nails seems to have made them stronger. The cleanup is a snap -- you just wipe them off and wash them occasionally, and they're quite durable. I did eventually break mine, but it was only by accidentally stepping on it. I've even sat on it plenty of times and had it hold up.So give them a try. You won't regret it.

Title: OPI Crystal Nail File
Text: As soon as I took this crystal file out of the package this baby went straight to work. This file works so effortlessly and gives a nice smooth finish unlike my metal nail file that always leaves my nails rough. I was pleased with the packaging also, especially since it comes in its own case. It's about the same size as a metal nail file but seems much more inconspicuous especially when I pull it out of my purse for a quick nail repair. I'll be using this crystal file a lot, we'll see how it holds up. This sleek and sophisticated OPI Crystal File is definitely my new favorite nail luxury.

Title: PediFix Ease Toe Pain, Toe Trainers
Text: I first used this product about two years ago. It fulfilled my expectations. I bought more toe trainers this year and they are a complete disappointment! The strap becomes separated very fast, so much so that the velcro hurts my toes, and the whole thing stretches after using it a few times. I will not recommend you waste your money on this product. I am back to using tape!

Title: Denco Easy Grip Professional Foot Smoother ~ Callus Shaver ~ w/Lifetime Warranty
Text: Nice but ....pricey...at 15.95...Denco Easy Grip Professional Foot Smoother ~ Callus Shaver ~ w/Lifetime Warranty...Works fine for the one rough patch on my right foot.

Title: Pedifix Double-Toe Straightener #P57
Text: Dispensed by podiatrists, these special pads work well for MOST people with crooked toes. However, if you have very RIGID toe problems, while this particular device is probably still worth testing, try also pedifix toe cushions, toe caps or toe separators. And don't be dismayed, even a podiatrist will require some product 'trial and error' to find the best solution for YOUR particular condition. No Sore Feet! FS

Title: MAXIM 575B Professional Manicure/Pedicure Machine 4 in 1 kit
Text: i really like this. im still learning to use the drill, and i think it should have better instructions. i have not had a problem with it, yet. i think it needs better instructions on what bit is for what. i dont think its very clear on how to change the bits either, but i figured it out. it was cheaper than the other drills, althoug i bought it for 59.99, two days later it was 39.99, and the customer service wasnt very helpful.

Title: HoMedics Man-170 StyleSpaDeluxe Manicure System
Text: I am a customer from England and have found all the customer reviews on this product extremely helpful as I am looking for a suitable home pedicure/manicure system to use in between visits to a professionally trained chiropodist/podiatrist and manicurist.Nailcare and care of the hands and feet is extremely important as all kinds of injuries can lead to greater health problems/infections (especially for persons with diabetes).My impression of this product is that it has very useful attachments (more so than the Revlon system which I purchased in the UK).I can see the point about it being like a dentist's instrument. As a dental surgery assistant I agree that it is very similar to the kind of attachments that we use for filing and shaping teeth - especially prosthetics - in the surgery, and must therefore be used with great care as the attachments could cause a lot of damage.I was tempted to buy the Homedics system as I thought that using the appliance as gently as possible might be useful - but the comment about the appliance being difficult to charge up/recharge for use has made me decide not to purchase this particular appliance.I hope that the manufacturers of this appliance will take note of their customers' comments and attempt to produce a much better product.Bearing in mind my comments about damage to the extremeties (hands and feet) it could be a potentially dangerous product for diabetic patients if injury occurs with its use.

Title: Satin Edge Manicure/Pedicure Set 9 pieces
Text: The set is nicely made , but a little "clunky" due to it's size. The clippers would be easier to use if they were a little smaller.

Title: HoMedics Man-170 StyleSpaDeluxe Manicure System
Text: It started working fine after waiting forever to charge it for the first time. Won't charge anymore and won't work! Didn't get much use out of it either! Not worth the price!

Title: Body Toolz Electric Manicure Pedicure Kit
Text: I AM HANDICAPPED- CAN'T CUT MY NAILS ANY LONGER- MY HUSBAND FILES MY TOE NAILS DOWN - AND I FILE MY FINGERNAILS DOWN- AM SO HAPPY WITH MY PEDICURE SET--THANK YOU VERY MUCH-MRS. DELORES ROSENKILDE

Title: Bliss Diamancel Diamond Nail File No.2, Medium
Text: I really like this file. It shapes my nails quickly and does not leave jagged edges. My problem is the price. It is not worth $28. I ended up paying $18 because of a multi-item discount and that seems fair.

Title: HoMedics MAN-50C StyleSpa Traveler Manicure Kit
Text: If you pay more than $10 for this you paid too much. It has almost no power at all. The only good uses for this is to keep it in your desk at work or use as a quick fix kit until you can a manicure from a professional or home to your better manicure set. I let my 4 year old use it as a toy, so she can act just like mommy while I use a better kit.

Title: Jerdon Pedismooth Pedicure Set
Text: It works very good, efficient, good potency. It is better to removes the calluced skin. If you are diabetic, it's a very good solution to avoid accident with the blade-cutting-callous. I recommended this set.

Title: Pedifix Pedifix 3-Layer Toe Separators
Text: My husbands toes feel like they are cramped in shoes. These toe separators are perfect for him. His toes dont bother him at all when he has these on. Perfect

Title: Pro-Tec Athletics Toe Flexor
Text: I get gout and other feet related issues for someone being 550lbs and this REALLY HELPS. Especially now I upped my work outs to lose weight my feet/toes kill me typically after do these things and now i put these on and yes my feet feel as if what are you doing to me at 1st but my feet have never felt better in a long time.

Title: Moom Beauty Products VOLCANIC BEAUTY STONE
Text: not much to say, it holds up is decent size and has a nice grit not too fine.

Title: Body Toolz Deluxe Manicure/Pedicure Kit
Text: beautifully made set in a leather case with everything one would need. I bought 2 one as a gift which was very well received.

Title: DIAMANCEL Foot Buffer (#11 Medium)
Text: Oh My! This product lives up to its fab reviews. From the minute I used it, it began transforming the rough patches and calluses on my feet into smooth skin. Remember to use on DRY skin. I can throw out my pumice stone! I HIGHLY recommend this item!

Title: Aromatherapy Herbal Slippers - Warm Whiskers Lavender Heatable Pink Bunny Slippers - Small
Text: These were a gift and my friend just loved them. We both work out of our homes and I was always talking about working in my rabbit slippers.So I got her some and she loves them. When you heat the pad up in the microwave they are so cozy.

Title: Body Toolz Deluxe Manicure/Pedicure Kit
Text: I bought this for my college age niece. She was very please with the manicure kit packaging and the quality of the supplies.

Title: Nutranail Bullet-Professional Strength .38 oz.
Text: I've been in love with the stuff for years. My nails endure a lot of abuse from typing, gardening, frequent handwashing, and handling of various kinds of equipment. Also, I pick at them. As a result, they are usually stubby, painfully short and peeling. Nutranail Bullet-Proof is the only thing that will protect my nails long enough for them to grow to a reasonable length. I can't fault it in any way. You just have to try it!

Title: China Glaze Nail Lacquer with Hardeners
Text: Got this for the fact it was neon, not opaque enough, didn't like it....very speedy fast shipping though, very weird formula too

Title: China Glaze Nail Lacquer with Hardeners
Text: I ordered this nail polish because I was looking for a different brand from the OPI brand. The shipping was super fast. However I did happen to notice upon further review that the color online looks different from the actual product. I have not used the polish yet but I still like it even though the color online looks brighter than the actual product once I got it.

Title: China Glaze Nail Lacquer with Hardeners
Text: Color is bright, especially in the light! Just a little thin, takes a lot of coats 3-4 and doesn't last as long as other colors. Maybe it's the neon? But all in all I love it.

Title: Nailtek Extend Professional Polish Thinner, 0.5 Fluid Ounce
Text: This product is great for thinning out Nail Tek products and helps it last longer. I haven't used it on regular nail polish, but I'm sure it would work.

Title: Zoya Nail Polish
Text: i found this nail polish in a pregnancy magazine, and though i'd give it a try for health reasons, but even better than that, the colors are beautiful, they go on perfectly even, have high shine, and last at least 5 days... even through endless hand washing and diaper changing. i highly recommend them!

Title: OPI Soft Shades Nail Lacquer
Text: this in the spotlight pink is pictured a very pink color. but it is a very very sheer barely pink. its more like a clear topcoat. i brushed on 4 coats before it looked a very pale hint of pink shade. i usually dont like to use more than 2 coats because the polish doesnt seem to last as long before it begins to chip up off the edges, the thicker the polish the more easily it peels up or chips,... so i only gave this a 3 star rating. but with the 4 coats it is a nice barely subtle pink, for a casual clean look. just like a shiny topcoat really.

Title: Essie Spring '06 Collection
Text: The Moonstruck is my favorite neutral Essie color. My order came quick and the polishes were in perfect condition. Definitely look forward to being a regular costumer.

Title: China Glaze Nail Lacquer with Hardeners
Text: It reminds me of one of the new Camaro colors...which is why I choose it. The polish itself is a little thick but still, the color is phenomenal!

Title: China Glaze Nail Lacquer with Hardeners
Text: I thought this would have a more nude tone but it is pink. More like blush than naked, but I love it regardless!

Title: OPI Start-to-Finish Base Coat, Top Coat & Strengthener
Text: Works great as all three. Great base coat, really does smoothe out nail surface and make a good base for polish. As a top coat, makes polish last longer. I wouldn't use polish without it!

Title: China Glaze Nail Lacquer with Hardeners
Text: I KNOW THAT WHEN I WORE THIS NAIL POLISH IT WOULD BE NOTICED AND A NUMBER OF PEOPLE WOULD WANT TO KNOW WHERE I GOT IT BECAUSE THEY TO WOULD LIKE IT ALOT

Title: Essie Spring '06 Collection
Text: This nail polish is a good one. But I was expecting more of it. First of all, I say it is very expensive so I thought it would be a great quality, I wanted it for french nails. but for my suprise it came to my house with a thick consistency. I don't know if all essey nail polish are like this, but this was my first essey nail polish and all I can say is that I prefer buying the inexpensive revlon I have always buyed.

Title: Zoya Nail Polish
Text: Bought this for my daughter and she just loves the color. I would suggest this color to anyone that loves medium blue color.

Title: OPI Katy Perry Collection Nail Lacquer
Text: i love what it does! but before you use it, try to watch the tutorial for the product on youtube to prevent making newbie mistakes. i'm looking forward to the silver shatter.

Title: Essie Spring '06 Collection
Text: This is good, but not as pink as I'd hoped. It was a little chalkier looking and I ended up using a different Essie color. I had ordered it for my wedding, but have used it other times for different days.

Title: China Glaze Nail Lacquer with Hardeners
Text: Im not sure why, but the seller has a picture of a completely different polish color up for flip flop fantasy.This color is actually a bright neon coral color. Look it up on google images before buying to make sure you know what your getting. I did, and I love this color! It's awesome for summer, and looks great with my tan skin tone. VERY BRIGHT. As in neon bright. Definetly a fun color.However, you need to apply atleast 2-3 coats for it to look even and nice. Also, after it dries you need to apply a coat of nail shine or else it will look flat. The nail shine made it much better! Im not sure why they made it so flat. It's probably not one of China Glaze's best polishes in my opinion, because of how it applies.

Title: China Glaze Nail Lacquer with Hardeners
Text: This is the first time I bought China glaze and I LOVE this color!! The picture doesn't do justice! This color is a lot brighter pinkish/red then the picture makes it out to be!Super Cute! Went on nice! Def. buying more of this brand!

Title: China Glaze Nail Lacquer with Hardeners
Text: I was looking for the perfect TARDIS blue color fingernail polish and I found it in Anchors Away. I was literally ecstatic when I found it on Amazon, so I ordered it right away. When it came in, I immediately tried it out and was even more happier than I had imagined with my purchase. China Glaze is wonderful polish that lasts quite a while and doesn't chip as easily as most normal polishes would. It's the perfect polish for any Doctor Who fan or anyone who enjoys polish in general.

Title: Essie Blanc 10 - Nail Polish / Lacquer / Enamel
Text: I ordered this item and it arrived damaged. I had to write 2 emails until I received a response. The second got me a response that said they will send a new bottle. After 2 weeks, no nailpolish. I contacted them again but no word. I had to file a claim through Amazon. Amazon contacted them and they issued a refund. Not worth the effort, better suppliers I'm sure exist.

Title: Russian Traditional 9 pc / 9 in Nesting Nested Stacking doll s-109
Text: My 10 yr old daughter wanted a set of these. She loves them. These are FAR superior to any set you'd find in a toy store. The biggest doll is HUGE, and they seem to go on and on (which is the fun part) until they get to the tinniest little one you can imagine. The dolls are wonderfully detailed and hand painted. Even the shipping box they came in was a joy for her. It was covered in Russian writing (machine and hand printed) and tied with twine just like old fashion parcels were. Plus the idea of getting mail from halfway around the world was a thrill for her. Opening the box and finding a Russian dime included was the cherry on top.I figured this would be the type of purchase that would get "played" with for a day and forgotten, but she loves them and carries them everywhere. The real authentic Russian touches are what really made this worth it. I'm sure if I had just grabbed a mass produced set from a toy store they would have been forgotten by now. We are thrilled with our purchase decision.

Title: White Nights Russian Nesting Doll 5pc./6"
Text: A fondness for nested dolls can escalate to a craving. It becomes too easy to choose, purchase and even gift-wrap a matryoshka such as "White Nights" - - and then - - realize you cannot give it away, even to the most favorite of friends. Be grateful if this has not happened to you!You might start saving money and vow to share generously with someone a nesting doll just as precious. "White Nights" is a visual delight and is educational about a subject you may have thought would never interest you. Since the early 1700's when St. Petersburg was founded by Czar Peter I, the city has been an impressive cultural center.The five nested dolls, each 'scarved' in white, display hand-painted scenes of city landmarks including "The Church of Our Saviour on the Spilled Blood." The author HENRI NOUWEN first encountered Rembrandt's oil painting of "The Prodigal" in the St. Petersburg gallery "The Hermitage". To learn more about this link to the historic city, look up Nouwen's book

Title: Duck Brand 393482 Softex Shower Mat, 21 X 21-Inch, White
Text: I love this shower mat. It actually stays put in the shower! This mat is so soft on your feet. It almost makes you want to buy 10 more to put all over the bathroom floor.

Title: Duck Brand 393482 Softex Shower Mat, 21 X 21-Inch, White
Text: This mat does a really good job, and dries quickly, without mildewing. One sligt drawback is that you can feel the suction cups under your feet. (I have neuropathy, which makes my feet sensitive, so this may not be an issue with most folks).

Title: Aquatopia Deluxe Safety Easy Bath Kneeler, Blue
Text: This simple little bath mat is a knee saver. I can spend alot more time and be alot closer wtih my little ones while giving them a bath. Money worth spent.

Title: Pipedreams Nipple Erector Set
Text: These will live up to all expectations IF the target nipples meet a couple simple criteria.They are best on nipples that already get hard on their own. The harder the better. Particularly soft nipples, or ones with scar tissue that inhibits blood flow, simply will not form the pressure on the end to keep these on. I should mention that the harder the nipples, the more painful these rubber gaskets will be also. There's a happy medium somewhere in there depending on the wearer.Tapering nipples make less of a difference than I expected. As long as they're about... 4+ times as long as the bands are wide they shouldn't roll off.If it doesn't sound like this will fit you, look for "sweater bumpers" or consider using the pump or similar item just to suck on the target nips regularly. This can increase the blood flow to the area and possibly cause longer term firmness.Edit

Title: Pipedreams Nipple Erector Set
Text: nice set....I was glad to see it was made of hard plastic.....I got a set from another seller and the suction tubes were glass and I put them in my pocket and one broke not very happy

Title: Sportsheets Hide Your Vibe Pillow, Black
Text: I love this pillow. I have six toys and they all fit in this pillow. I wanted somewhere to keep my toys clean, and out of sight. Living in a dorm with a close-minded roommate, privacy is limited and there aren't many places for me to keep them from her snooping. This soft little pillow hides them great and people have even laid on the pillow and didn't even know anything was inside. Zipper is a little flimsy, just be gentle with it.

Title: Kama Sutra Oil of Love, 3.4 Ounces
Text: I had a tiny bottle of this and tried it on myself, by myself, and I don't know what is in it but it works great, I mean grrrrrrrrrrreaaaaaaaaat~! love it--who needs a man!

Title: The Original Fart Machine #2 - Remote Fart Machine
Text: When I bought it I new I was going to enjoy it very much with friends, but it did not came close to the real fun it is too much....

Title: Doggie Done It - Fake Poo
Text: They are a bit smaller then I imagined but whatever. People/hippies have started to install fake grass around my neighborhood so I've been buying these and placing them on the fake grass. Fake poop on fake grass = funnyhaha.If you walk by you can notice it fine and you will laugh but they are a little too small to be noticed and appreciated by people driving by.

Title: Forum Novelties 63503 Amazing Gag Shock Lighter
Text: It broke after a single use. I tried to pull out the battery tray but it just broke into pieces and tore a wire out. Terrible quality.

Title: One Pack of Shocking Gum, Funny Shock Gag (Random Color)
Text: Good but the label gives it away (gum reads

Title: Realistic Full Size Rubber Mouse Halloween Gag
Text: I placed this on the kitchen counter, back against the wall - and then exclaimed "What's THAT?" pointing to the mouse. Got an awesome reaction from the woman of the house!

Title: The Original Fart Machine #2 - Remote Fart Machine
Text: I ordered the fart machine from amazon and recieved it in 3 days. We have had more fun with this fart machine. I'm not sure what's more fun ... making it look like someone else did it or being the farter yourself. I enjoyed letting it rip and politely saying excuse me.

Title: Shocking Duel Action Game
Text: woot was selling these one day during a woot off, and they looked so damn interesting, I ended up buying the maximum of 3 of these units for the discounted price they had going. My friend and I faced off with 1 of the units and decided it wasn't brutal enough, so we threw batteries into a 2nd unit and were facing off with a shock joystick in each hand. This made things far more interesting, and we decided to create a drinking game incorporating this fun little toy. Here are the rules we've come up with. We're expecting great fun at parties to come!The SHOCKING drinking game.(to separate the men from the boys)2 people face off and grab the shock controllers when signaled by the time keeper/announcer and they will start a stopwatch. The game begins with a staring contest. Individuals will turn toward their spotter and drink when specified and go back to staring when specified. Drinks will be taken through a straw for the duration of the drink period. If you blink during or turn away during the staring portion you lose. If you fail to drink during a drinking portion, you lose. If you let go of either of the shock joysticks during the game at any time, you lose. Consequence of loss will be determined before the beginning of the game.0:20 DRINK!0:25 STARE!0:40 DRINK!0:45 STARE!1:00 DRINK!1:05 STARE!1:20 DRINK!1:25 STARE!1:40 DRINK!1:45 STARE!2:00 DRINK!2:05 STARE!Have fun, we are!

Title: SHOCKING PEN - EMPIRE EDITION
Text: Got this for my son, took it out of packaging and wrapped it like an ordinary pen, LOL, he was pretty surprised and we had a good laugh. Ofcourse my 8 year old then realizing it was a prank pen got his mom and grandparents to write down something, and ZAP(not that old). We have had a blast with this inexpensive classic.

Title: Forum Novelties 63503 Amazing Gag Shock Lighter
Text: first one didn't work, replacement worked a few times but weak(barely noticed). batteries are tiny button cells that can't be replaced. anyway you'll be lucky to open up & put back together without it breaking/falling apart. junk.

Title: Party Pooper - Fake Poop
Text: Come on people! Don't you know the difference between poop and crap?? The turd length/turd width ratio (also known as the s... ratio) is consistent with that of crap, not poop. Crap has a shiny finish, poop is dull in appearance. Before we can ever hope to have an intelligent conversation about the Party Pooper Fake Poop, we must first get the nomenclature right!What really makes this a piece of crap is the convex shape. The curve kills the prank. When you place it on something that is not a toilet seat, it looks like the turd is trying to crawl away like a worm. You can see daylight underneath it! I've never seen poop like that. I'm telling you, this is not poop! It's crap!The prank worked on the kids, but the wife knew it was fake right away. Let's see what kind of trouble I can get into with this thing at work....

Title: Schylling Tin Elephant on a Bike
Text: I was under the impression the ball on the top of the elephant would turn. It does not. I think it would be a much cooler toy if that part moved. No directions come with the toy. NO way of knowing if the ball was supposed to move or not. Bummed out. It is just a paper weight now.

Title: 'Stink Bombs' - Practical Joke by Loftus International
Text: These stink bombs smell like rotten eggs and the reaction people get from this smell is hilarious! The smell doesn't last very long but the few minutes that it does is worth it because of the faces people get when they smell the stink bomb which makes you laugh so hard!

Title: Forum Novelties 63503 Amazing Gag Shock Lighter
Text: Too easy to tell it is not a real lighter. Stupid or drunk people might be the only times people will fall for this one.

Title: Schylling Tin Elephant on a Bike
Text: I teach kids with autism, and this toy is a very systematic toy. I can use it to teach them to request, say go, and use it as a reinforcer. The ball on the top of the elephants nose did turn while the elephant goes in circles. The only thing is that the toy seems very fragile, I would not let my students play with it unattended! But other than that--i love it!

Title: Schylling Rocket Ride Tin Collectible
Text: ... so much fun, I didn't want to wrap it up! Great construction and craftsmanship, with a vintage appeal. The moving parts are a wonder, all without batteries (!)

Title: Rubber Razzer (Fart Whistle)
Text: I think that the title speaks for itself. Fun idea for boys, but, does not last any time at all, and they all laughed because it sounds like a goose instead of... well what it is supposed to sound like. I would say, avoid.

Title: One Pack of Shocking Gum, Funny Shock Gag (Random Color)
Text: We bought this thinking it would make people laugh. Instead it made them angry. The fact that the packaging warns against using against kids under 14 and adults over 50, limits the audience kids can play trick on. With that kind of disclaimer, this seems more like a weapon than a gag.Nobody laughs when you shock them with this. We threw it away immediately.

Title: Mystic Smoke
Text: This product is horrible, It doesn't wash off your fingers or hands, When i had tried to get a little of the substance out of the tube(with alot of pressure), the bottom blew out and it started coming through the bottom of the tube, it also takes a while for the smoke to start appearing, you need alot of the substance on your fingers in order for a good affect, don't get me wrong it looks cool but it's a pain in the ass to get off, i was very disappointed.

Title: Schylling Rocket Ride Tin Collectible
Text: Just purchased this for my 8 year old son who loves amusement parks. It works great and very good quality. I feel this is definitely a toy that he will cherish forever and take care of. I would highly recommend this product.

Title: Harmonic Spinning Top, Farm by Bolz
Text: I wanted to give one of these to my niece's daughter for a while. She really likes it!

Title: Boppy Luxe - Clouds
Text: I think it is not comfertable around my waist and I had a 29-30 waist my babys body took the whole thing up and he was 6lb 8oz and 18in.(he was small and so am I) so I was not very happy with it. So I go a different breastfeeding pillow that is just wonderful that I would give a 6 star out of 5 stars, it is red with bears on rocking chairs(I would have prefered clouds on thi one) and it was even cheaper than boppy. also perchaced at babys r us. but everybody is different so try someone elses and see if it works for you. it just wasn't for me but it was for my friend so you never know till you try it yourself.

Title: Symphony-In-Motion Mobile
Text: Our son was really freaked out by this for a long time, he would stare at it in terror if we started playing it. Then I got a couple of the characters from this mobile that I could hang from his activity arch so he got used to them. Now he likes the mobile a lot, he will even "talk" at it while its playing. I don't use it a bed time, but if I need to put him down in his crib for 15-20 minutes while I do something, this keeps him amused to whole time.

Title: Badger Basket Three Basket Set
Text: I love these baskets! They are a pale pink with beautiful details including white lining and lace over the top edges.

Title: Boppy Luxe - Clouds
Text: My mother's a lactation consultant and bought this for me. I was having major problems breastfeeding and this was wonderful. My daughter and I had to use the football hold for the first two months or so and this was an absolute lifesaver. Now that she's bigger and we've advanced to the traditional breastfeeding position, we don't use it for nursing. But it's great to prop her in and let her see what's going on. It's a nice alternative to her swing. And the fleece cover is so soft and comfy. As a sign that she'd outgrown her diapers, one of her poops leaked out onto the cover and the pillow. Both the cover and the pillow washed great in the washer & dryer. The Boppy is great!

Title: Lambs & Ivy Sweet as a Daisy Lamp with Shade
Text: I love the night light and lamp combination. It is a terrific space saver. It is just right for a baby and a toddler. Will not be outgrown too quickly as a baby lamp would.

Title: Symphony-In-Motion Mobile
Text: All I can say is that when my son is fussy, I put him in his crib and turn on the mobile, he is completely entranced by the cute little muppets and the fun shapes. I am so happy with it!

Title: Soothing Saranade
Text: We absolutely loved this mobile and actually just took it out of our daughters room. Love the remote feature!! The fact that the mobile arm part removes and turns into a music box is ingenius!!! Great feature for safety reasons when she learned to stand. Also our daughter loved taking the pieces to teeth on because they fit perfectly in little hands. All the fabric pieces also wash up nicely in the washing machine so that is another added bonus. WELL WORTH THE MONEY PAID!!!

Title: DEX Products Sound Sleeper SS-01
Text: I give this product 2 stars rather than one because I really did like the selection of sounds and the option to combine them. The unit itself was small and lightweight enough to travel with. However, the sound quality was terrible. I almost wonder if I got a defective product, or if they are really just that bad. The timer/on/off switch made a static noise whenever I moved it, almost like it had a short. The highest volume setting was surprisingly quiet. For a product that is all about making sound, the quality of the sound was pathetic.

Title: Malden Metal Picture Frame
Text: Very cute frame! My friend was all gaga over the picture of her cute baby in the cute frame. Just be careful cutting the picture to fit the frame as it has a bit of a funky shape that the photo has to fit into.

Title: Wooden Growth Stick
Text: I absolutely love the way this looks in my house. It was a little tough to get hung at exactly the right height. We also are still trying to figure out the right kind of pen to use to write on it. But we love the way it looks, and that it is portable - you're not stuck with where you keep your kids' measurements in the house.

Title: Symphony in Motion Mobile - Geometric
Text: This is the best mobile ever. I had a sweet little sailboat mobile that went with the nursery. I got tired of having to wind it up every 2 minutes. I got this mobile to "try it out". The baby loves it. I got smiles from a 1 months old when I turned it on. Now, if he is sleepy he will fall asleep watching it. If he is wide awake - he just stares at it and smiles and coos. Can't say enough wonderful things about this mobile.

Title: DEX Products Sound Sleeper SS-01
Text: I needed something to cover road sounds at night. This has done everything it said it would. I will even be able to use it for my grandchildren if need be.

Title: Boppy Luxe - Clouds
Text: I breastfed my daughter until she was a year old and found the Boppy to be pretty much useless. It wasn't the right height for nursing for me and I preferred to use a smaller pillow which conformed to my daughter's body better.We used it once or twice for sitting up, but most of the time, she was supported by my legs or multiple pillows. I think this is one of those products that's either love/hate--for us, it has to be the latter!

Title: Sweet JoJo Designs Musical Baby Crib Mobile
Text: This might be a great product except for the small fact that I can't use it at all because it will not attach to the crib.

Title: The First Years - Crib CD Player
Text: As with other reviewers, I agree that this CD player eats batteries. However, I use the timer on it and it works much better than just allowing it to run all night. I disagree with the reviewers who contend that the night light is weak or dim. I think it is a perfect light; who really wants a super-bright light shining on their baby when they are trying to get them to sleep anyway? I have had no problems with the CD's skipping. The only reason I give this product 3 stars instead of more is because of the rate at which it uses batteries; I'd prefer the batteries last longer but, again, using the timer has helped even with that complaint.

Title: Cars Victory Lane Blue/Black/Grey Self-Stick Wall Border, 5-Inch x 15-Foot
Text: This border is self stick, extremely easy to put on the wall, and looks great!No issues with peeling or falling off. Best part is, my son loves it!

Title: Mascotopia Sports Team Crib Mobile
Text: The item is as advertised and is really cool looking, the issue is that when you wind it up it only operates for about 3 minutes, not even close to enough time to put my boy to sleep. I am going to try to find a cheap mobile and harvest the rotator part and replace the one that came with the mobile.

Title: DEX Products Sound Sleeper SS-01
Text: My husband has slept with a noisy CPAP machine since 1992. When it broke, and couldn't be fixed, we had to buy a new one. It's quiet! That might sound like a good thing, but I was used to the "white noise." I bought this machine, and it works wonderfully. There are a lot of different sounds, but I have picked out the one that sounds the most like white noise to me. The volume of the sound can easily be raised or lowered - it's great! I would buy this again. P.S. It has a timer on it that I don't care for, so if you don't want to time your sounds, be sure you don't push the button all the way up to the timer.

Title: Symphony-In-Motion Mobile
Text: This is a great mobile...my 3 month old loves the music, loves the motions, and we can always hear he "talking" to the moving animals. She really loves it...just be prepared with a LOT of batteries! And I mean a lot! And yes the motor is loud...But she loves it so much and doesn't seem to mind the squeeking.
